creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks outlines five pillars for deploying AI agents at enterprise scale: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The framework emerged from a retail bank engagement where six weeks of evaluation and tracing infrastructure were needed before model selection, and post-launch monitoring caught stale data issues when policy changes occurred.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Agents often fail silently when web access is blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or stale training data while claiming to have searched the web. Real web access via MCP protocols with proper rendering and anti-bot handling improves reliability, citation accuracy, and reduces hallucinations in agent responses.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA accelerated video diffusion generation to near real-time on a single Blackwell B200 by combining dynamic quantization, latent caching that skips recomputation between denoising steps, and step distillation using student-teacher training with 1-8 steps instead of 50. The approach achieves 10-200x speedup and is packaged in the open source FastGen framework.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: Double iframe pattern nests an inner srcdoc iframe inside an outer iframe loaded from a controlled subdomain to simultaneously enforce Content Security Policy, prevent storage collisions, and avoid sandbox escapes. The technique originated in Facebook's app marketplace and is now used by MCP and ChatGPT apps to securely isolate third-party code while maintaining necessary browser APIs.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use loop-based orchestration to automate agent prompting without manual intervention. The approach chains agents together with an orchestrator that spawns parallel workers for tasks, storing observations in a database for monitoring and reliability. This technique reduces manual prompting but increases token costs and can compound hallucinations without proper observability harnesses.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness for orchestrating multiple coding agent models (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers within a single system. It enables workflow patterns where different models handle different stages of tasks, provides a governance layer for human-in-the-loop control, and maintains session continuity across devices.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 was withdrawn from availability within three days of launch reportedly due to a US government export-control directive related to alleged jailbreak vulnerabilities. The video discusses the regulatory decision, its impact on developers building on LLMs, and benchmark comparisons with prior Claude versions.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides agents with identity, sandboxed execution for generated code, and audit trails, alongside a skills-based architecture compatible with various AI coding assistants like Claude.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer and provides setup guidance. The tutorial includes a GitHub template repository and workshop for building AI agents using the Loop Engineer framework.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explaining how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems using AI loops. Covers autonomous systems that can optimize ads and SEO through continuous feedback and improvement mechanisms.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and Goal-buddy capabilities in what he characterizes as the arrival of true autonomous coders. The video covers how the /goal feature works, its setup, prompting practices, and Mission exploration for long horizontal tasks.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews OpenAI Symphony, a new AI coding framework or paradigm. The video covers what Symphony is, how it works, harness setup with auto testing, and WORKFLOW.md configuration for getting started.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Video explains Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model as an introduction to how language models process tokens. Covers tokenization, embedding, vocabulary, cross entropy loss, softmax, optimization with AdamW, and backpropagation as prerequisites for understanding GPT and attention mechanisms.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The models combine hybrid mamba transformer, Mixture of Experts in latent space (Latent MoE), and MTP architecture. The video analyzes how these architectural components differentiate Nemotron 3 from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a large language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA), which replaces full attention with sparse attention patterns. The model includes tiled reads and optimized KV cache handling to improve performance during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Pi, an agent framework with a unique harness layer that enables flexible deployment patterns. The video compares Pi against other frontier agent tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex in terms of architectural complexity and use cases.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with curated tools, documentation, and guardrails for AWS service interaction. It provides up-to-date knowledge and best practices to improve agent success rates and reduce inefficiency.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers tutorial on deploying Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock to maintain data residency and compliance. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, team cost tracking, and guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses strategies for job hunting in 2026 as AI screens resumes, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open source contributions over mass-applying. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation, drawing parallels to how jobs evolve rather than disappear with technological shifts.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five production deployment patterns for TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsiveness, layered authentication combining API Gateway and IAM, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The guidance emphasizes moving beyond localhost development to handle real-world requirements like scale, security, and cost control.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video exploring whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" (likely referring to AI-assisted or intuition-driven development). The content compares OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities and examines adoption trends at Google across 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or service related to open-source software. The video explores tensions between the company's approach and traditional open-source maintenance, framing the impact as potentially transformative or disruptive to the open-source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that despite a coming "ChatGPT Moment" for robotics, the field's focus on humanoid form factors is myopic. She contends that designing robots for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, will be more important as billions of robots enter society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into a single interface for enterprise decision-making. Founded by Eléonore Crespo, it has raised over $400 million and counts Figma, Brex, and Anthropic as customers, growing 2x year over year.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses distinguishing real product-market fit from false signals. He argues that passionate early customers do not prove PMF, emphasizes pull signals over push, and advocates for embedding in customer workflows rather than conducting formal interviews. He also recommends betting on AI model trajectory rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, discusses his decision to leave competitive sailing and build an ad monetization platform for AI tools, raising $26M. He argues that ads rather than subscriptions are the sustainable monetization model for AI applications, and identifies human trust as the bottleneck for AI agent adoption, not technical capability.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
